passages.title: First Snow
passages.text:
1. The first snow of winter drifted quietly over the neighborhood during the night. By morning, rooftops, sidewalks, and tree branches were covered in a blanket of white. The world seemed brighter, as sunlight sparkled across the fresh snow.

2. Children bundled themselves in coats, scarves, and gloves before stepping outside. Their footprints crisscrossed the untouched snow, creating winding paths across yards and sidewalks. Laughter echoed through the cold air as snowballs flew and sleds glided down gentle hills.

3. Nearby, smoke curled from chimneys, and warm yellow light glowed from house windows. The scent of hot chocolate drifted from open doors as families returned indoors to warm up after spending time in the crisp winter air.

4. For a few hours, the neighborhood felt transformed. Familiar streets and houses seemed different beneath the layer of snow, creating a scene that felt peaceful, playful, and new.

passages.title: Summer Evening at the Lake
passages.text:
5. The lake was calm as the sun began to sink below the horizon. Golden light stretched across the water, turning the surface into a sheet of shimmering color. Gentle waves rolled toward the shore.

6. Families gathered along the beach, spreading blankets and sharing picnic dinners. Children searched for smooth stones while others skipped them across the water. The sound of conversation mixed with the chirping of crickets and the distant call of birds returning to their nests.

7. A warm breeze rustled the leaves of nearby trees. Boats drifted slowly across the lake as their reflections shimmered in the fading sunlight. The sky gradually changed from blue to shades of orange, pink, and purple.

8. As darkness approached, the lake grew quieter. The peaceful scene invited visitors to pause, relax, and enjoy the beauty of the evening before heading home.

passages.title: A Life of Helping Others
passages.text:
1. Clara Barton is remembered as one of the most caring and determined people in American history. Although she lived many years ago, her actions continue to inspire people today. She spent much of her life helping others during difficult times and worked to improve the lives of people in need.

2. Clara Barton was born in Massachusetts in 1821. As a child, she enjoyed learning and helping those around her. When she grew older, she became a teacher. At a time when many schools had limited resources, Clara worked hard to provide students with opportunities to learn and succeed.

3. During the American Civil War, Clara noticed that many soldiers were injured and needed supplies. She collected food, clothing, blankets, and medicine to deliver to those who were hurt. She often traveled to dangerous areas to make sure soldiers received the help they needed.

4. Because of her dedication, many people began calling her the "Angel of the Battlefield." Clara did not seek recognition for her work. Instead, she focused on helping as many people as possible. Her kindness and courage earned the respect of soldiers, doctors, and community leaders.

5. After the war, Clara learned about an international organization called the Red Cross. The organization provided aid to people affected by disasters and emergencies. Clara believed that a similar organization could help people in the United States.

6. She worked for many years to make that idea a reality. In 1881, Clara Barton helped establish the American Red Cross. The organization provided assistance during floods, storms, and other emergencies. It also helped communities recover after disasters.

7. Clara continued supporting the American Red Cross for many years. Her leadership helped the organization grow and serve more people. Today, the American Red Cross still provides assistance to individuals and communities facing challenges.

8. Clara Barton's life shows how one person can make a difference through service and determination. Her efforts helped thousands of people during her lifetime, and her legacy continues to influence communities around the world.

one person can make a difference through service and determination

title: Kobe Bryant: A Legacy of Hard Work

passages.title: Kobe Bryant: A Legacy of Hard Work
passages.text:
1. Kobe Bryant is remembered as one of the greatest basketball players in history. During his twenty-year career with the Los Angeles Lakers, he became known for his skill, determination, and commitment to improving every day. His achievements on and off the basketball court continue to inspire people around the world.

2. Kobe Bryant was born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in 1978. As a child, he spent several years living in Italy while his father played professional basketball. During that time, Kobe developed a love for sports and practiced basketball whenever he could.

3. After returning to the United States, Kobe attended high school in Pennsylvania. He quickly became one of the nation's top basketball players. Instead of attending college, he entered the National Basketball Association (NBA) directly after high school. In 1996, he joined the Los Angeles Lakers.

4. Early in his career, Kobe worked hard to improve his skills. Coaches and teammates often described him as one of the most dedicated players they had ever seen. He spent countless hours practicing shooting, passing, and defensive techniques. His commitment to improvement helped him become one of the league's top players.

5. Throughout his career, Kobe earned many awards and achievements. He helped lead the Lakers to five NBA championships and was selected to play in numerous All-Star Games. Fans admired his ability to perform well during important moments and his determination to overcome challenges.

6. Kobe often spoke about the importance of perseverance and hard work. He believed that success comes from continuous effort and learning from mistakes. This mindset became known as the "Mamba Mentality," a phrase many people use to describe striving to be their best and never giving up.

7. After retiring from basketball in 2016, Kobe continued pursuing new goals. He supported youth sports, worked on creative projects, and encouraged young people to follow their dreams. He believed that the lessons learned through sports could help people succeed in many areas of life.

8. Kobe Bryant's life demonstrates the power of dedication and perseverance. Although he is remembered for his accomplishments in basketball, many people also admire the way he inspired others to work hard, face challenges, and pursue their goals with determination.

passages.text:
1. Eileen Gu is a world-class freestyle skier known for her skill, determination, and ability to balance many interests. She was born in California in 2003 and began skiing when she was very young. Her mother introduced her to the sport, and Eileen quickly developed a passion for it.

2. As she grew older, Eileen spent countless hours practicing jumps, spins, and tricks. Freestyle skiing requires athletes to perform difficult movements while racing down snowy courses. Success in the sport depends on both physical skill and mental focus.

3. Eileen's hard work paid off as she began winning competitions around the world. She became known for attempting challenging tricks that few athletes could perform. Even when training was difficult, she continued working toward her goals.

4. In addition to skiing, Eileen has always valued education. She worked hard in school and encouraged young people to pursue both academics and athletics. She often speaks about the importance of setting goals and staying committed to them.

5. Through her achievements, Eileen has inspired many young athletes. Her success demonstrates how dedication, preparation, and perseverance can help people reach ambitious goals.

passages.title: Alex Honnold Climbs New Challenges
passages.text:
6. Alex Honnold is a professional rock climber known for tackling some of the world's most difficult climbs. He was born in California in 1985 and developed an interest in climbing as a child. Over time, he became one of the most accomplished climbers in the sport.

7. Rock climbing requires strength, balance, and careful planning. Alex became famous for completing climbs that many people considered extremely challenging. Before attempting a climb, he often spent months studying routes and practicing each movement.

8. One of Alex's most well-known accomplishments was climbing El Capitan, a massive rock formation in Yosemite National Park. To prepare, he carefully planned every step and practiced repeatedly. His success showed the value of patience and preparation.

9. Alex believes that solving problems is an important part of climbing. Each route presents new obstacles that require focus and determination. He often explains that success comes from consistent effort rather than natural talent alone.

10. Beyond climbing, Alex supports projects that help communities gain access to clean energy. His work shows that people can use their success to make a positive impact on others.

2. One day, Sammie noticed colorful plastic bottle caps that were often thrown away. She learned that the caps could be recycled into benches. Inspired by this idea, Sammie began collecting bottle caps from friends, neighbors, and community members.

3. As more people joined her project, thousands of bottle caps were gathered. Sammie worked with others to turn the recycled materials into colorful benches. The benches were then donated to places where children and families could use them.

4. Sammie's efforts helped both people and the environment. The project reduced waste by recycling plastic materials, and it created cheerful places for families to sit and spend time together.

5. Her idea grew into a larger movement that encouraged communities to work together. Through determination and kindness, Sammie showed that one person's idea can inspire many others to take action.

7. Robby was born with a medical condition that caused him to experience many bone fractures throughout his life. Despite these difficulties, he remained energetic and optimistic. He believed that everyone could make a difference through kindness and encouragement.

8. With help from his family, Robby began creating videos that inspired people to help others, work together, and make their communities better places. His messages reached millions of viewers around the world.

9. Many schools, families, and community groups shared Robby's videos because they promoted positive actions and respect for others. His words encouraged people to look for ways to improve the lives of those around them.

title: The Day the Wright Brothers Flew

passages.title: The Day the Wright Brothers Flew
passages.text:
1. The wind blew steadily across the sandy dunes of Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, on December 17, 1903. Two brothers, Orville and Wilbur Wright, stood beside a machine they had spent years designing and building. Nearby, a few helpers watched as the brothers prepared for an experiment that many people believed was impossible.

2. The Wright brothers were not famous inventors. They owned a bicycle shop in Ohio and spent much of their free time studying how birds flew. They believed that if they could understand how birds balanced and moved through the air, they might be able to build a flying machine.

3. For years, the brothers tested ideas, built models, and made improvements. Many of their experiments failed. Some gliders crashed, while others did not perform as expected. Each setback taught them something new, and they continued working toward their goal.

4. On that cold December morning, the brothers rolled their aircraft into position. Orville climbed aboard while Wilbur steadied the machine. As the engine started, the aircraft moved forward across a wooden track.

5. Suddenly, the machine lifted off the ground. It flew for only twelve seconds and traveled about 120 feet, but it remained in the air under its own power. For the first time in history, a powered aircraft carrying a person had completed a controlled flight.

6. The brothers were thrilled. Throughout the day, they completed several more flights, each one traveling farther than the last. Their final flight covered more than 800 feet before strong winds damaged the aircraft.

7. At the time, few people realized how important the event would become. However, the Wright brothers had proven that powered flight was possible. Their achievement opened the door for future inventors and engineers to improve aircraft technology.

8. Today, airplanes connect communities across the world. People can travel long distances in a matter of hours, and goods can be transported quickly from place to place. These advances can be traced back to the determination and creativity of two brothers standing on a windy beach in North Carolina.

Answer: B

Answers: 1, 2, 3, 4

passages.title: Lost in the Grand Canyon
passages.text:
1. In 2019, thirteen-year-old Jordan Romero set out with his family to hike part of a trail near the Grand Canyon. The day began with clear skies and cool temperatures. Like many visitors, Jordan was excited to explore the steep cliffs, winding paths, and breathtaking views.

2. As the group hiked farther down the trail, Jordan stopped several times to take photographs of the canyon. At one point, he paused to examine an unusual rock formation. When he looked up, he realized that his family had continued around a bend in the trail and was no longer in sight.

3. Jordan hurried forward, expecting to catch up quickly. Instead, he reached a fork in the trail and was unsure which direction his family had taken. He called out, but no one answered. Suddenly, he found himself alone.

4. Jordan remembered advice he had learned during outdoor safety lessons. Instead of wandering farther, he decided to stay calm and think carefully. He moved to a safe location near the trail and looked for ways to make himself easier to find.

5. The afternoon grew warmer, and Jordan became thirsty. Fortunately, he had packed a water bottle before leaving. He took small sips and avoided unnecessary movement to conserve energy. He also used a whistle attached to his backpack to signal for help every few minutes.

6. Meanwhile, park rangers and Jordan's family began searching the area. Rangers knew that people who stay in one place are often found more quickly than those who continue moving. They searched the trails and listened for sounds that might help them locate him.

7. Several hours later, a ranger heard Jordan's whistle echo through the canyon. Following the sound, the ranger found him sitting safely beside the trail. Jordan was tired, but he was unharmed.

8. Afterward, Jordan reflected on the experience. He realized that staying calm, following safety guidelines, and making thoughtful decisions had helped him stay safe until help arrived. His adventure became a reminder that preparation and clear thinking can be just as important as courage when facing a challenge.

questions.text:

A. He continued hiking until he found a ranger station.B. He stayed calm, remained near the trail, and followed safety guidelines.C. He climbed to a higher location to search for his family.D. He left the trail to look for a shortcut.
Answer: B

preparation and clear thinking can help people overcome challenges

passages.title: Trapped by the Tide
passages.text:
1. Twelve-year-old Ben loved exploring tide pools along the rocky coast near his hometown. One summer afternoon, he joined his uncle on a walk to search for crabs, sea stars, and other marine animals that appeared when the tide was low.

2. As they explored, Ben became fascinated by a group of tide pools farther down the shore. He carefully climbed across several rocks to get a closer look. While he was observing the animals, he lost track of time.

3. When Ben finally looked up, he noticed that the ocean was much closer than before. The tide was rising quickly, and water now covered many of the rocks he had crossed earlier. He realized that returning the same way could be dangerous.

4. Instead of panicking, Ben remembered advice his uncle had given him. He moved to a high, stable rock above the water and stayed where he could be seen. Then he used a bright orange jacket to signal for help.

5. Ben's uncle soon noticed that he was missing. After searching the shoreline, he spotted the orange jacket waving above the rocks. A local rescue team helped Ben return safely to shore.

6. Ben later explained that he had wanted to solve the problem by himself. However, staying calm and making safe decisions helped him avoid greater danger. The experience taught him that preparation and patience can be important survival skills.

passages.title: Lost in the Snow
passages.text:
7. Eleven-year-old Maya was participating in a family snowshoeing trip in the mountains. Fresh snow covered the landscape, and tall pine trees stretched across the hills. The scenery was beautiful, but it also made it difficult to see landmarks.

8. While taking photographs, Maya accidentally fell behind the group. When she tried to catch up, she discovered that several sets of tracks crossed the trail. She was no longer sure which direction her family had gone.

9. Maya felt worried, but she remembered what she had learned during an outdoor safety program. She stopped walking and looked for a sheltered area protected from the wind. Then she used her whistle to make noise at regular intervals.

10. Maya also put on an extra layer of clothing from her backpack to stay warm. She knew that conserving energy and protecting herself from the cold were important while waiting for help.

11. Searchers located Maya later that afternoon after hearing her whistle. Although she had been frightened, she remained safe because she followed the survival steps she had practiced before the trip.

12. Looking back, Maya believed that staying calm was the key to her success. By using the knowledge she had learned ahead of time, she was able to make smart decisions during a challenging situation.

questions.text:
What theme is shared by both passages?
A. Exploring alone is the best way to learn new skills.B. Staying calm and using safety knowledge can help people handle difficult situations.C. Rescue teams can solve every problem without help from others.D. Outdoor adventures are too dangerous to enjoy.
Answer: B

Which details from the passages support the idea that preparation helped Ben and Maya stay safe?
☑ Ben remembered advice his uncle had given him and moved to a safe location.☑ Ben used his bright orange jacket to signal for help.☑ Maya remembered what she learned in an outdoor safety program.☑ Maya used a whistle and put on an extra layer of clothing while waiting for help.☐ Ben enjoyed exploring tide pools and observing marine animals.
Answers: 1, 2, 3, 4

Answers:

finding shelter, staying warm, and using a whistle

title: The Midnight Ride of Paul Revere

passages.title: The Midnight Ride of Paul Revere
passages.text:
1. The streets of Boston were quiet on the night of April 18, 1775. Most people were asleep, unaware that an important event was about to take place. However, Paul Revere was awake and preparing for a dangerous journey.

2. Leaders of the American colonies had learned that British soldiers might be marching toward nearby towns. The soldiers were expected to seize supplies and arrest colonial leaders. Revere's mission was to warn people that the troops were coming.

3. Mounting his horse, Revere rode into the darkness. He traveled along winding roads, stopping at houses and meeting places to share the news. Along the way, he alerted colonists who would continue spreading the warning throughout the countryside.

4. As he rode, Revere knew that British patrols were searching the area. If he were caught, he could be arrested. Despite the risk, he continued his journey because he believed the message was too important to ignore.

5. By the next morning, many colonists had received the warning. Although Paul Revere was not the only rider carrying messages that night, his ride became one of the most famous events leading up to the American Revolution. His actions helped prepare communities for the challenges ahead.

passages.title: Amelia Earhart's Flight Across the Atlantic
passages.text:
6. More than 150 years later, another American prepared for a different kind of journey. In 1932, Amelia Earhart climbed into a small airplane and prepared to fly alone across the Atlantic Ocean.

7. At the time, long-distance flights were difficult and dangerous. Pilots faced changing weather, mechanical problems, and limited navigation tools. Few people had successfully completed such a journey alone.

8. Earhart's flight began smoothly, but challenges soon appeared. Strong winds pushed against the aircraft, and icy conditions formed on parts of the plane. Throughout the trip, she remained focused on reaching her destination safely.

9. Hour after hour, Earhart guided the airplane across the ocean. She relied on her training, experience, and determination to continue moving forward despite the obstacles she encountered.

10. When she finally landed in Ireland, Amelia Earhart became the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean. Her achievement inspired people around the world and demonstrated the power of courage and perseverance.

11. Today, both Paul Revere and Amelia Earhart are remembered for taking on difficult challenges. Although their journeys were very different, each showed how determination can influence history.

questions.text:
How are Paul Revere and Amelia Earhart similar?
A. Both traveled by airplane across the ocean.B. Both completed dangerous journeys that required courage and determination.C. Both warned communities about approaching danger.D. Both lived during the American Revolution.
Answer: B

Which details from the passages support the idea that both individuals persevered through challenges?
☑ Paul Revere continued his ride even though British patrols were searching for him.☑ Revere believed his warning was too important to ignore.☑ Amelia Earhart faced strong winds and icy conditions during her flight.☑ Earhart relied on her training and determination to continue despite obstacles.☐ Earhart landed in Ireland after crossing the Atlantic Ocean.
Answers: 1, 2, 3, 4

Complete the comparison.
In The Midnight Ride of Paul Revere, Paul Revere demonstrates courage by [continuing his warning mission despite the risk of being caught / flying across the Atlantic Ocean alone]. In Amelia Earhart's Flight Across the Atlantic, Amelia Earhart demonstrates courage by [continuing her flight despite difficult weather and conditions / delivering messages to colonial communities].
Answers:
continuing his warning mission despite the risk of being caught
continuing her flight despite difficult weather and conditions
